msgs.text:
Turn 92 — standalone appendix artifact and verifier core I continued from the frozen Turn 91 state. The attached history’s latest direction was to stop the obstruction sweep and package the proof as a certificate appendix: fixed orientation ρS=rS−1, right-row convention, Certificates 0–F, nonlinear contracts D.1–D.4, normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness descent. The history records the stronger target theorem as an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, descending to ΓS≅GΓH.I prepared the standalone appendix artifact and a deterministic verifier for the linear backbone:turn92_certificate_appendix.tex turn92_certificate_appendix.pdf turn92_linear_verifier.py turn92_linear_verifier_output.txt The PDF compiled successfully and rendered as a 4-page appendix. The verifier recomputes the primitive dimensionsdimL1,…,dimL10=2,3,2,6,6,11,18,36,56,105 and the boundary/secondary ranksd45678910dimLd6611183656105 rank∂d6918305699186 dimker∂d6346161324 rankθd05346151324dimHd(2)13712214381 matching the primitive-boundary backbone recorded in the sweep.

The appendix now states the proof as a certificate chain:

Certificate 0: fix ρS=rS−1 and right-row conventions.

Certificate A: use the H1 -Nielsen matrix U, with the Ef-piece row action (a,b)U0=(a(s−1)+b(s−2),a+2b),so that (s+3,−2)U0=(s2+1,s−1),det(U0)=s.Certificate B: use the reduced-coproduct primitive verifier for Ln=Primn(F2⟨A,B⟩).Certificate C: close the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1 tower via the uniform restricted-Lie recursion and Cm=Lm for m≥5.

Certificate D: freeze the four nonlinear finite blocks:ker∂8↠L5[2],degree-16 λ-toggle,rankD5,6,7=60, rank(D5,6,7,μ4B4)=61,θ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2].The D.3 rank jump 60→61 is the recorded certificate that the A2=R/(4,t), d=4 scalar obstruction is killed by the continuation kernel.Certificate E: normalize the Ef coefficient row exactly to (2,t), then close higher (2,t)-adic layers by the Koszul–Witt cascade.

Certificate F: use finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data SN,J, compactness, and the finite Frattini criterion to obtain a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, hence ΓS≅GΓH.

No new Magnus layer, coefficient quotient, A2 -subcase, or exceptional finite block was opened. The Turn 92 move is an artifact/preparation step: it packages the already-frozen proof and supplies an executable verifier for the deterministic linear part.

ESTABLISHED: Created a standalone certificate appendix and a working linear verifier. The appendix proves the frozen theorem: a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, hence ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. For a publication-grade appendix, the remaining external reproducibility additions would be the full nonlinear verifier scripts or pivot tables for D.1–D.4, especially D.1/D.2 and the D.3 60→61 stack-rank computation.

NEXT: Add the nonlinear verifier artifacts or explicit pivot tables under Certificate D; do not reopen the obstruction sweep.[TURN92-STANDALONE-APPENDIX-AND-LINEAR-VERIFIER]
